svg.text: even col = QA · odd col = QB
(physical J1 pin numbers per module manual)
Mega GND ↔ board GND — common ground, essential
Mega 5V → VCC — one-chip build only; larger populations get their own supply
Write data D — Mega drives (analog row as digital outputs)
Port chunks — 3 select bits + enable; green=bit0, red=enable
⚠ Mega drives WE, /OEA and /OEB — JOEA/JOEB jumpers must be OPEN
Read data — dark cyan QA (even row) · light cyan QB (odd row)
Pin-for-pin map — lowest pin in each chunk = its LSB (green/white wire)

g.text: nothing else shares the Q buses

svg.text: Dual-row rows = [5V] + 16 signals + [GND]; two 1×8 chunks fill each exactly. Any exposed pin means misalignment.
Address bit 3 of each port is jumper-strapped low in 8-register configurations (JW/JA/JB = 8R).
Register File Module — Arduino Mega 2560 Test Harness Hookup (matches RegisterFileTest.ino)
